---
title: "PCA_Plots"
output: html_document
date: "2025-04-09"
---

```{r}
    # Load necessary libraries 
    library(ggplot2)
    library(dplyr)
    library(SummarizedExperiment)
    library(DESeq2)             
    library(stringr)            
    library(patchwork)          

    # --- Standardization Settings ---
    # Define a common theme for all PCA plots
    common_pca_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) 
      theme(
        legend.position = "right",
        panel.grid = element_blank(),             
        axis.line = element_line(color = "black"), 
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = rel(1.2)), 
        axis.title = element_text(size = rel(1)), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = rel(1)),
        legend.text = element_text(size = rel(0.9)) 
      )

    # Define a common point size
    common_point_size <- 6
```


    
```{r}
   
 # --- RNA-seq PCA ---
    message("--- Processing RNA-seq Data ---")

    # --- USER: Adjust file path for the RAW count matrix RDS ---
    raw_counts_rds_file <- "count_matrix_RNAseq_MAGEL2_project.rds" # As named in original script

    if (!file.exists(raw_counts_rds_file)) {
      stop("RAW RNA-seq count matrix file not found: ", raw_counts_rds_file)
    }

    # --- Step 1: Load Raw Counts ---
    counts_RNA_subread <- readRDS(file = raw_counts_rds_file)
    counts_RNA <- counts_RNA_subread$counts # Extract counts matrix

    # --- Step 2: Clean Column Names ---
    original_colnames <- colnames(counts_RNA)
    for (i in 1:ncol(counts_RNA)) {
      colnames(counts_RNA)[i] <-
        str_replace(string = colnames(counts_RNA)[i],
                    pattern = ".bam",
                    replacement = "")

      colnames(counts_RNA)[i] <-
        str_replace(string = colnames(counts_RNA)[i],
                    pattern = "GM08330",
                    replacement = "8330")

      colnames(counts_RNA)[i] <-
        str_replace(string = colnames(counts_RNA)[i],
                    pattern = "MGH2069",
                    replacement = "MGH")

      colnames(counts_RNA)[i] <-
        str_replace(string = colnames(counts_RNA)[i],
                    pattern = "PSWT",
                    replacement = "PWST") # Corrected typo based on original code
    }
    message("Cleaned RNA-seq column names.")

    # --- Step 3: Create Metadata ---
    sample_info_rna <- data.frame(row.names = colnames(counts_RNA),
                                  background = c(rep("8330", 21), rep("MGH", 21)),
                                  genotype = c(rep("DelC", 3), rep("DupC", 3), rep("HetDel", 3),
                                               rep("HomoDel", 3), rep("WT", 3), rep("PWSTII", 3), rep("PWSTI", 3),
                                               rep("PWSTI", 3), rep("PWSTII", 3), rep("DelC", 3), rep("DupC", 3),
                                               rep("HetDel", 3), rep("HomoDel", 3), rep("WT", 3)),
                                  replicate = rep(c(1,2,3), 14))

    if (!all(rownames(sample_info_rna) == colnames(counts_RNA))) {
        stop("Mismatch between cleaned RNA column names and metadata rownames.")
    }

    sample_info_rna$background <- as.factor(sample_info_rna$background)
    sample_info_rna$genotype <- as.factor(sample_info_rna$genotype)
    sample_info_rna$replicate <- as.factor(sample_info_rna$replicate)
    message("Created RNA-seq metadata.")

    # --- Step 4: Create DESeqDataSet Object ---
    counts_RNA_int <- round(counts_RNA) # Ensure counts are integers
    dds_RNA <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts_RNA_int,
                                      colData = sample_info_rna,
                                      design = ~ background + genotype)
    message("Created DESeqDataSet object.")

    # --- Step 5: Apply Variance Stabilizing Transformation ---
    vsd_RNA <- vst(dds_RNA, blind = TRUE)
    message("VST transformation complete.")

    # --- Step 6: Perform PCA ---
    expression_matrix_rna <- assay(vsd_RNA)
    pca_rna <- prcomp(t(expression_matrix_rna), scale. = TRUE)
    pca_rna_df <- data.frame(pca_rna$x, colData(vsd_RNA)) # Combine PCA results and metadata

    # Calculate variance explained
    pca_rna_var <- pca_rna$sdev^2
    pca_rna_var_perc <- round(pca_rna_var / sum(pca_rna_var) * 100, 1)

    # Axis labels
    xlab_rna <- paste0("PC1 (", pca_rna_var_perc[1], "%)")
    ylab_rna <- paste0("PC2 (", pca_rna_var_perc[2], "%)")

  # --- Step 7: Generate Standardized Plots ---
# Plot 1: Colored by genotype
pca_rna_plot_genotype <- ggplot(pca_rna_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = genotype, color = genotype)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.7) +
  common_pca_theme +
  xlab(xlab_rna) +
  ylab(ylab_rna) +
  ggtitle("RNA-seq PCA by Genotype") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21, alpha = 0.7))) # Match legend to points

# Plot 2: Colored by background
pca_rna_plot_background <- ggplot(pca_rna_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = background, color = background)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.7) +
  common_pca_theme +
  xlab(xlab_rna) +
  ylab(ylab_rna) +
  ggtitle("RNA-seq PCA by Background") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21, alpha = 0.7))) # Match legend to points

# Display or save the plots
print(pca_rna_plot_genotype)
print(pca_rna_plot_background)

# Optional: Combine plots using patchwork
rna_combined_plot <- pca_rna_plot_genotype + pca_rna_plot_background +
  plot_layout(guides = 'collect') + # Collect legends together
  plot_annotation(title = 'RNA-seq PCA Analysis', theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face="bold")))
print(rna_combined_plot)
```
 RNAseq: PC3
```{r}
# Add a sample column if useful for labeling
pca_rna_df$sample <- rownames(pca_rna_df)

# Label for PC3
pc3_lab <- paste0("PC3 (", pca_rna_var_perc[3], "%)")

# Plot PC3 only, colored by genotype
pca_rna_plot_pc3 <- ggplot(pca_rna_df, aes(x = genotype, y = PC3, fill = genotype, color = genotype)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.7, shape = 21) +
  common_pca_theme +
  xlab("Genotype") +
  ylab(pc3_lab) +
  ggtitle("RNA-seq PCA: PC3 scores by Genotype") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21, alpha = 0.7)))

print(pca_rna_plot_pc3)

# Label for PC3
pc3_lab <- paste0("PC3 (", pca_rna_var_perc[3], "%)")

# Optional sample names
pca_rna_df$sample <- rownames(pca_rna_df)

# All samples in one column
pca_rna_plot_pc3_one_column <- ggplot(
  pca_rna_df,
  aes(x = "All samples", y = PC3, fill = genotype, color = genotype)
) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.08, size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.7, shape = 21) +
  common_pca_theme +
  xlab("") +
  ylab(pc3_lab) +
  ggtitle("RNA-seq PCA: PC3") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21, alpha = 0.7)))

print(pca_rna_plot_pc3_one_column)
```
 
 
       
```{r}
# --- Proteomics PCA ---
# Adapted from user-provided working example

# Load necessary libraries (ensure these are installed)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(SummarizedExperiment) # For assay()
library(patchwork)          # For combining plots (optional)

message("--- Processing Proteomics Data (User-Provided Logic) ---")

# --- Standardization Settings (Copied for standalone use) ---
# Define a common theme for all PCA plots
common_pca_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) + # Set base font size
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),             # Remove grid lines
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"), # Add axis lines
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = rel(1.2)), # Centered, bold title
    axis.title = element_text(size = rel(1)), # Axis titles
    legend.title = element_text(size = rel(1)), # Legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = rel(0.9)) # Legend text
  )

# Define a common point size
common_point_size <- 6


# --- USER: Adjust file paths ---
proteomics_rds_8330 <- "new_data_imp_8330_BH_new.rds"
proteomics_rds_MGH <- "new_data_imp_MGH_BH_new.rds"
proteomics_metadata_rds <- "proteomics_sample_info.rds"

if (!file.exists(proteomics_rds_8330) || !file.exists(proteomics_rds_MGH) || !file.exists(proteomics_metadata_rds)) {
  stop("One or more Proteomics input files not found.")
}

# --- Step 1: Load Data ---
new_data_imp_8330 <- readRDS(file = proteomics_rds_8330)
new_data_imp_MGH <- readRDS(file = proteomics_rds_MGH)
# Rename variable to avoid potential conflicts if run with other code
experimental_design_prot <- readRDS(file = proteomics_metadata_rds)

# --- Step 2: Prepare Data ---
# Extract abundance data and gene names
abundance_8330 <- assay(new_data_imp_8330)
abundance_MGH <- assay(new_data_imp_MGH)
genes_8330 <- rownames(abundance_8330)
genes_MGH <- rownames(abundance_MGH)

# Find common genes
genes_overlap_prot <- intersect(genes_8330, genes_MGH)
message(paste("Found", length(genes_overlap_prot), "common genes/proteins."))

# Subset abundance matrices to common genes
abundance_8330_overlap <- abundance_8330[genes_overlap_prot, , drop = FALSE] # Use drop=FALSE
abundance_MGH_overlap <- abundance_MGH[genes_overlap_prot, , drop = FALSE] # Use drop=FALSE

# Prepare sample information (filter metadata)
sample_info_8330_prot <- experimental_design_prot[experimental_design_prot$background == "GM", ]
sample_info_MGH_prot <- experimental_design_prot[experimental_design_prot$background == "MG", ]

# Combine abundance data using simple cbind (relies on implicit order)
# NOTE: This assumes the column order in the original subsetted matrices
# matches the row order of the filtered metadata.
abundance_combined_prot <- cbind(abundance_8330_overlap, abundance_MGH_overlap)
message(paste("Combined abundance matrix dimensions:", nrow(abundance_combined_prot), "rows,", ncol(abundance_combined_prot), "cols"))

# Combine sample information
sample_info_combined_prot <- rbind(sample_info_8330_prot, sample_info_MGH_prot)
message(paste("Combined metadata dimensions:", nrow(sample_info_combined_prot), "rows"))


# Ensure column names match metadata sample names by assigning them
# NOTE: This overrides original column names and might mask ordering issues.
if (ncol(abundance_combined_prot) == nrow(sample_info_combined_prot)) {
    colnames(abundance_combined_prot) <- sample_info_combined_prot$name
    message("Assigned column names from metadata to combined abundance matrix.")
} else {
    stop(paste("Column count mismatch:", ncol(abundance_combined_prot), "cols in abundance vs", nrow(sample_info_combined_prot), "rows in metadata. Cannot assign names."))
}

message("Prepared combined proteomics data.")

# --- Step 3: Perform PCA ---
# NOTE: Using the logic from the user-provided snippet.
# This version does NOT filter for zero-variance features.
# The 'cannot rescale a constant/zero column' error might reappear if
# zero-variance features exist in this combined dataset.

# Ensure no missing values in the data matrix intended for PCA
if (any(is.na(abundance_combined_prot))) {
    stop("Missing values (NA) detected in the proteomics abundance matrix. Imputation or filtering needed before PCA.")
    # Add alternative NA handling here if needed (imputation, removal)
}

# Check if any rows remain (should generally be true unless overlap is empty)
if (nrow(abundance_combined_prot) == 0) {
    stop("No proteins/genes in the combined matrix (common overlap is empty?). Cannot perform PCA.")
}
# Check if enough columns (samples) remain
if (ncol(abundance_combined_prot) < 2) {
    stop("Fewer than 2 samples in the combined matrix. Cannot perform PCA.")
}

# Perform PCA directly on the combined data
message("Performing PCA on combined proteomics data...")
# Using variable names from user snippet for PCA result and data frame
pca_combined <- prcomp(t(abundance_combined_prot), scale. = TRUE)

# Combine PCA results with the sample info
# Using variable names from user snippet
pca_df <- data.frame(pca_combined$x, sample_info_combined_prot)

# Calculate variance explained
pca_prot_var <- pca_combined$sdev^2
pca_prot_var_perc <- round(pca_prot_var / sum(pca_prot_var) * 100, 1)

# Axis labels
xlab_prot <- paste0("PC1 (", pca_prot_var_perc[1], "%)")
ylab_prot <- paste0("PC2 (", pca_prot_var_perc[2], "%)")

# --- Step 4: Generate Standardized Plots ---
# Plot 1: Colored by condition
pca_plot_condition <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = condition, color = condition)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.7) + # Use fillable shape & transparency
  common_pca_theme +
  xlab(xlab_prot) +
  ylab(ylab_prot) +
  ggtitle("Proteomics PCA by Condition") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21, alpha = 0.7))) # Match legend to points

# Plot 2: Colored by background
pca_plot_background <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = background, color = background)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.7) + # Use fillable shape & transparency
  common_pca_theme +
  xlab(xlab_prot) +
  ylab(ylab_prot) +
  ggtitle("Proteomics PCA by Background") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21, alpha = 0.7))) # Match legend to points

# Display the plots
print(pca_plot_condition)
print(pca_plot_background)

# Optional: Combine plots using patchwork
prot_combined_plot <- pca_plot_condition + pca_plot_background +
  plot_layout(guides = 'collect') +
  plot_annotation(title = 'Proteomics PCA Analysis', theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face="bold")))
print(prot_combined_plot)
# ggsave("Proteomics_PCA_Combined.png", prot_combined_plot, width = 10, height = 5)

message("--- Proteomics PCA Plot Generation Complete ---")
```




```{r}
# --- Ubiquitylation PCA ---
# Adapted using the same logic as the reverted Proteomics section

# Load necessary libraries (ensure these are installed)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(SummarizedExperiment) # For assay()
library(patchwork)          # For combining plots (optional)

message("--- Processing Ubiquitylation Data (User-Provided Logic) ---")

# --- Standardization Settings (Copied for standalone use) ---
# Define a common theme for all PCA plots
common_pca_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) + # Set base font size
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),             # Remove grid lines
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"), # Add axis lines
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = rel(1.2)), # Centered, bold title
    axis.title = element_text(size = rel(1)), # Axis titles
    legend.title = element_text(size = rel(1)), # Legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = rel(0.9)) # Legend text
  )

# Define a common point size
common_point_size <- 6

# --- USER: Adjust file paths ---
ubi_rds_8330 <- "Ubi_data_imp_8330_BH_new.rds"
ubi_rds_MGH <- "Ubi_data_imp_MGH_BH_new.rds"
ubi_metadata_rds <- "UbiEAs_sample_info.rds" # Corrected filename from original user code

if (!file.exists(ubi_rds_8330) || !file.exists(ubi_rds_MGH) || !file.exists(ubi_metadata_rds)) {
  stop("One or more Ubiquitylation input files not found.")
}

# --- Step 1: Load Data ---
Ubi_data_imp_8330 <- readRDS(file = ubi_rds_8330)
Ubi_data_imp_MGH <- readRDS(file = ubi_rds_MGH)
experimental_design_ubi <- readRDS(file = ubi_metadata_rds)

# --- Step 2: Prepare Data ---
# Extract abundance data and site names (assuming rows are sites/peptides)
abundance_8330_ubi <- assay(Ubi_data_imp_8330)
abundance_MGH_ubi <- assay(Ubi_data_imp_MGH)
sites_8330_ubi <- rownames(abundance_8330_ubi)
sites_MGH_ubi <- rownames(abundance_MGH_ubi)

# Find common sites/peptides
sites_overlap_ubi <- intersect(sites_8330_ubi, sites_MGH_ubi)
message(paste("Found", length(sites_overlap_ubi), "common ubiquitylation sites/peptides."))


# Subset abundance matrices to common sites
abundance_8330_overlap_ubi <- abundance_8330_ubi[sites_overlap_ubi, , drop = FALSE] # Use drop=FALSE
abundance_MGH_overlap_ubi <- abundance_MGH_ubi[sites_overlap_ubi, , drop = FALSE] # Use drop=FALSE

# Prepare sample information (filter metadata)
# Assuming 'background' column uses "GM" and "MG" codes
sample_info_8330_ubi <- experimental_design_ubi[experimental_design_ubi$background == "GM", ]
sample_info_MGH_ubi <- experimental_design_ubi[experimental_design_ubi$background == "MG", ]

# Combine abundance data using simple cbind (relies on implicit order)
# NOTE: This assumes the column order in the original subsetted matrices
# matches the row order of the filtered metadata.
abundance_combined_ubi <- cbind(abundance_8330_overlap_ubi, abundance_MGH_overlap_ubi)
message(paste("Combined ubiquitylation matrix dimensions:", nrow(abundance_combined_ubi), "rows,", ncol(abundance_combined_ubi), "cols"))


# Combine sample information
sample_info_combined_ubi <- rbind(sample_info_8330_ubi, sample_info_MGH_ubi)
message(paste("Combined ubiquitylation metadata dimensions:", nrow(sample_info_combined_ubi), "rows"))


# Ensure column names match metadata sample names by assigning them
# NOTE: This overrides original column names and might mask ordering issues.
if (ncol(abundance_combined_ubi) == nrow(sample_info_combined_ubi)) {
    colnames(abundance_combined_ubi) <- sample_info_combined_ubi$name
    message("Assigned column names from metadata to combined ubiquitylation matrix.")
} else {
     stop(paste("Column count mismatch:", ncol(abundance_combined_ubi), "cols in abundance vs", nrow(sample_info_combined_ubi), "rows in metadata. Cannot assign names."))
}

message("Prepared combined ubiquitylation data.")

# --- Step 3: Perform PCA ---
# NOTE: Using the logic adapted from the user-provided snippet.
# This version does NOT filter for zero-variance features.
# The 'cannot rescale a constant/zero column' error might reappear if
# zero-variance features exist in this combined dataset.

# Ensure no missing values in the data matrix intended for PCA
if (any(is.na(abundance_combined_ubi))) {
    stop("Missing values (NA) detected in the ubiquitylation abundance matrix. Imputation or filtering needed before PCA.")
    # Add alternative NA handling here if needed (imputation, removal)
}

# Check if any rows remain (should generally be true unless overlap is empty)
if (nrow(abundance_combined_ubi) == 0) {
    stop("No ubiquitylation sites in the combined matrix (common overlap is empty?). Cannot perform PCA.")
}
# Check if enough columns (samples) remain
if (ncol(abundance_combined_ubi) < 2) {
    stop("Fewer than 2 samples in the combined ubiquitylation matrix. Cannot perform PCA.")
}

# Perform PCA directly on the combined data
message("Performing PCA on combined ubiquitylation data...")
pca_combined_ubi <- prcomp(t(abundance_combined_ubi), scale. = TRUE)

# Combine PCA results with the sample info
pca_ubi_df <- data.frame(pca_combined_ubi$x, sample_info_combined_ubi)

# Calculate variance explained
pca_ubi_var <- pca_combined_ubi$sdev^2
pca_ubi_var_perc <- round(pca_ubi_var / sum(pca_ubi_var) * 100, 1)

# Axis labels
xlab_ubi <- paste0("PC1 (", pca_ubi_var_perc[1], "%)")
ylab_ubi <- paste0("PC2 (", pca_ubi_var_perc[2], "%)")

# --- Step 4: Generate Standardized Plots ---
# Plot 1: Colored by condition
pca_ubi_plot_condition <- ggplot(pca_ubi_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = condition)) +
  geom_point(size = common_point_size) + # Use common standardized size
  common_pca_theme +                     # Use common standardized theme
  xlab(xlab_ubi) +
  ylab(ylab_ubi) +
  ggtitle("Ubiquitylation PCA by Condition")

# Plot 2: Colored by background
pca_ubi_plot_background <- ggplot(pca_ubi_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = background)) +
  geom_point(size = common_point_size) + # Use common standardized size
  common_pca_theme +                     # Use common standardized theme
  xlab(xlab_ubi) +
  ylab(ylab_ubi) +
  ggtitle("Ubiquitylation PCA by Background")

# Display the plots
print(pca_ubi_plot_condition)
print(pca_ubi_plot_background)

# Optional: Combine plots using patchwork
ubi_combined_plot <- pca_ubi_plot_condition + pca_ubi_plot_background +
  plot_layout(guides = 'collect') +
  plot_annotation(title = 'Ubiquitylation PCA Analysis', theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face="bold")))
print(ubi_combined_plot)

message("--- Ubiquitylation PCA Plot Generation Complete ---")
```


```{r}
# --- Add-on: RNA-seq PCA with Marginal Densities (WT vs. Mutant) ---
message("--- Generating RNA-seq Marginal Density Plot ---")

# Load the new library
library(ggExtra)

# --- Step 1: Create the WT vs. Mutant Grouping ---
# Uses the 'pca_rna_df' created by your script
pca_rna_df_grouped <- pca_rna_df %>%
  mutate(group = factor(ifelse(genotype == "WT", "WT", "Mutant")))

# --- Step 2: Create the Base Scatter Plot ---
# We map the new 'group' column to 'color'
# (Using xlab_rna and ylab_rna from your script)
p_rna_base <- ggplot(pca_rna_df_grouped, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.7) +
  common_pca_theme +
  ggtitle("RNA-seq PCA (WT vs. Mutants)") +
  xlab(xlab_rna) +
  ylab(ylab_rna) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") # Use distinct colors for groups

# --- Step 3: Generate and Print the Marginal Density Plot ---
rna_marginal_plot <- ggMarginal(
  p_rna_base,
  type = "density",
  groupColour = TRUE, # Tell ggMarginal to use the 'color = group' mapping
  groupFill = TRUE,   # Tell ggMarginal to fill the densities
  alpha = 0.4         # Set transparency for the density fills
)

print(rna_marginal_plot)
# ggsave("RNAseq_PCA_Marginal_Density.png", rna_marginal_plot, width = 8, height = 8)


# --- Add-on: Proteomics PCA with Marginal Densities (WT vs. Mutant) ---
message("--- Generating Proteomics Marginal Density Plot ---")

# Load the new library
library(ggExtra)

# --- Step 1: Create the WT vs. Mutant Grouping ---
# Uses the 'pca_df' created by your script
pca_prot_df_grouped <- pca_df %>%
  mutate(group = factor(ifelse(condition == "WT", "WT", "Mutant")))

# --- Step 2: Create the Base Scatter Plot ---
# We map the new 'group' column to 'color'
# (Using xlab_prot and ylab_prot from your script)
p_prot_base <- ggplot(pca_prot_df_grouped, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.7) +
  common_pca_theme +
  ggtitle("Proteomics PCA (WT vs. Mutants)") +
  xlab(xlab_prot) +
  ylab(ylab_prot) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") # Use distinct colors for groups

# --- Step 3: Generate and Print the Marginal Density Plot ---
prot_marginal_plot <- ggMarginal(
  p_prot_base,
  type = "density",
  groupColour = TRUE, # Use the 'color = group' mapping
  groupFill = TRUE,   # Fill the densities
  alpha = 0.4         # Set transparency
)

print(prot_marginal_plot)
# ggsave("Proteomics_PCA_Marginal_Density.png", prot_marginal_plot, width = 8, height = 8)




# --- Add-on: Ubiquitylation PCA with Marginal Densities (WT vs. Mutant) ---
message("--- Generating Ubiquitylation Marginal Density Plot ---")

# Load the new library
library(ggExtra)

# --- Step 1: Create the WT vs. Mutant Grouping ---
# Uses the 'pca_ubi_df' created by your script
pca_ubi_df_grouped <- pca_ubi_df %>%
  mutate(group = factor(ifelse(condition == "WT", "WT", "Mutant")))

# --- Step 2: Create the Base Scatter Plot ---
# We map the new 'group' column to 'color'
# (Using xlab_ubi and ylab_ubi from your script)
p_ubi_base <- ggplot(pca_ubi_df_grouped, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.7) +
  common_pca_theme +
  ggtitle("Ubiquitylation PCA (WT vs. Mutants)") +
  xlab(xlab_ubi) +
  ylab(ylab_ubi) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") # Use distinct colors for groups

# --- Step 3: Generate and Print the Marginal Density Plot ---
ubi_marginal_plot <- ggMarginal(
  p_ubi_base,
  type = "density",
  groupColour = TRUE, # Use the 'color = group' mapping
  groupFill = TRUE,   # Fill the densities
  alpha = 0.4         # Set transparency
)

print(ubi_marginal_plot)
# ggsave("Ubiquitylation_PCA_Marginal_Density.png", ubi_marginal_plot, width = 8, height = 8)
```


```{r}
# --- RNA-seq: Combined PCA (Genotype + Background) ---

# Define the shape mapping: 8330 = ▼ (fillable triangle), MGH = ● (fillable circle)
shape_map_rna <- c("8330" = 25, "MGH" = 21)

pca_rna_combined_plot <- ggplot(pca_rna_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = genotype, color = genotype, shape = background)) +
  # Map 'color' to 'genotype' and remove static color setting
  geom_point(size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Apply the custom shape mapping
  scale_shape_manual(values = shape_map_rna, name = "Background") +
  
  # Label the fill legend correctly
  scale_fill_discrete(name = "Genotype") +
  # Add a matching color scale
  scale_color_discrete(name = "Genotype") +
  
  # Apply the common theme
  common_pca_theme +
  xlab(xlab_rna) +
  ylab(ylab_rna) +
  ggtitle("RNA-seq PCA by Genotype and Background") +
  
  # Tidy the legend
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21, size = 5)),
    color = "none" # Hide the redundant color legend
  )

print(pca_rna_combined_plot)
```


```{r}
# --- Proteomics: Combined PCA (Condition + Background) ---

# Define the shape mapping: GM = ▼ (fillable triangle), MG = ● (fillable circle)
shape_map_prot <- c("GM" = 25, "MG" = 21)

pca_prot_combined_plot <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = condition, color = condition, shape = background)) +
  # Map 'color' to 'condition'
  geom_point(size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Apply the custom shape mapping
  scale_shape_manual(values = shape_map_prot, name = "Background") +
  
  # Label the fill legend correctly
  scale_fill_discrete(name = "Condition") +
  # Add a matching color scale
  scale_color_discrete(name = "Condition") +
  
  # Apply the common theme
  common_pca_theme +
  xlab(xlab_prot) +
  ylab(ylab_prot) +
  ggtitle("Proteomics PCA by Condition and Background") +
  
  # Tidy the legend
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21, size = 5)),
    color = "none" # Hide the redundant color legend
  )

print(pca_prot_combined_plot)
```


```{r}
# --- Ubiquitylation: Combined PCA (Condition + Background) ---

# Define the shape mapping: GM = ▼ (fillable triangle), MG = ● (fillable circle)
shape_map_ubi <- c("GM" = 25, "MG" = 21)

pca_ubi_combined_plot <- ggplot(pca_ubi_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = condition, color = condition, shape = background)) +
  # Map 'color' to 'condition'
  geom_point(size = common_point_size, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Apply the custom shape mapping
  scale_shape_manual(values = shape_map_ubi, name = "Background") +
  
  # Label the fill legend correctly
  scale_fill_discrete(name = "Condition") +
  # Add a matching color scale
  scale_color_discrete(name = "Condition") +
  
  # Apply the common theme
  common_pca_theme +
  xlab(xlab_ubi) +
  ylab(ylab_ubi) +
  ggtitle("Ubiquitylation PCA by Condition and Background") +
  
  # Tidy the legend
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21, size = 5)),
    color = "none" # Hide the redundant color legend
  )

print(pca_ubi_combined_plot)
```
